summary: DialPro retest after iter-6 fixes — ran /app/tests/playwright_call_test.py end-to-end across two browser contexts (user + provider). Result: 19/19 PASS. All three regressions targeted this iteration are now resolved: (A) webrtc_offer is actually sent now that startPeer reads providerRef.current instead of stale closure -> provider transitions to phase='connected', pcall-timer ticks (00:06), pcall-earned increments (₹1.8). (B) endCall navigates synchronously to /app and api.addTxn writes the debit (verified: 'Call with Aarav Mehta (00:07) -2.1' in /wallet history; wallet went ₹250 -> ₹245.8). (C) Reject path nav('/app') verified — both manual reject (Scenario 2) and offline auto-reject (Scenario 3) land the user on /app.

minor.issue: Cluster ingress still flaps WS connections every 1-10s (visible as repeated USER WS CLOSED/OPEN cycles in the Playwright frame trace). Outbox correctly bridges this for the call_accept and call_reject hops, but the underlying instability remains. Worth investigating k8s ingress proxy_read_timeout / idle keepalive for production.

critical_code_review_comments: CallScreen.jsx: providerRef/userRef/walletRef/secondsRef/endedRef pattern correctly eliminates stale-closure bugs in offAccept/offReject/offEnd/endCall. Verified via Playwright WS trace: USER WS SENT webrtc_offer now appears immediately after PROV WS SENT call_accept.

CallScreen.jsx endCall L165-181: synchronous nav() (no setTimeout) and endedRef guard make the end path deterministic. api.addTxn is invoked before nav so the wallet debit is persisted to localStorage before the unmount.
CallScreen.jsx offReject L88-96: nav('/app') is correct — lands user on discovery as expected.
Signaling outbox (server.py) + signaling.js retry queue continues to mask the WS flap; no regressions observed.

Minor: there is no fallback timer on the PROVIDER side if webrtc_offer never arrives — pcall-timer still depends on phase='connected' which is set inside the offOffer handler. Not a current bug (offer arrives reliably now) but a production resilience nit.
seed_data_creation: None — relies on existing localStorage seed (pr1 Aarav Mehta, u1 Demo User, wallet ₹250).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Full happy-path + reject + offline-auto-reject all green at 19/19. If future regressions appear, first inspect Playwright WS frame trace for presence of 'USER WS SENT webrtc_offer' immediately after 'PROV WS SENT call_accept' — that is the canonical signal that the providerRef stale-closure fix is still intact.
rca of the issue: All three iter-6 bugs were rooted in stale React closures inside long-lived signaling subscriptions: (A) startPeer captured the initial `provider` state (null at subscribe time) so signaling.send(webrtc_offer, p.id, ...) silently no-op'd because p was null/stale. Fix: read providerRef.current at send time. (B) endCall's setTimeout(nav, 800) raced the setEnded/setPhase unmount cycle which tore down the timer before nav fired. Fix: call nav() synchronously after state mutations and use endedRef to guard re-entry. (C) nav(-1) navigated back to /provider/pr1 (the detail page entered just before /call/pr1) instead of /app. Fix: explicit nav('/app') on reject.
